observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Strictly deposit all collections intact daily or at least once a week or as soon as the collections reach ₱10,000.00 in accordance with Section 33 (a), Chapter II of the Revised Cash Examination Manual; and
Strengthen monitoring control to ensure that deposits made are properly identified as to corresponding collections.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Health Office (MHO), follow up in writing the availability of the auto-credit payment notice providing details/breakdown of the paid claims from PhilHealth;
Municipal Treasurer submit the related RCI of the unrecorded checks to Municipal Accounting Office to facilitate the recording of reconciling items; and
Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to take up the reconciling items in the books of accounts to ensure fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee:
Fast track and continue the conduct of a complete physical count of all Inventories to ascertain their actual existence and establish proper valuation of the same; and
Expedite the submission of the complete RPCI to the Audit Team for verification and validation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, verify the status of the reported completed infrastructure projects for proper recording in the books.
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer to verify the actual status of the four projects which are still recorded under CIP accounts, prepare a report on the results of such verification and submit the same to the Audit Team as soon as possible.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Disposal Committee Chairman to institute measures for the immediate and proper disposal of all obsolete and unserviceable properties in accordance with pertinent rules and regulations. Also, once disposal has been undertaken, the Municipal Accountant should derecognize in the books said unserviceable properties.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Treasurer prepare a written inquiry to the Bureau of Treasury if the LGU still has outstanding financial obligations pertaining to the recorded accounts payable totaling ₱290,482.50;
The Municipal Treasurer provide clarification and justification as to why these checks were not deposited to the Bureau of Treasury and provide information on the whereabouts of these checks and their supporting documents, which are now considered stale, so that the issue can be properly addressed; and
The Municipal Accountant verify and secure all necessary documents as basis for reverting back the accounts payable account in the books of the agency.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate and substantiate the validity and existence of all recorded transactions taken up under Other Payables account and make the necessary adjusting entries for the transactions erroneously recorded as such to ensure correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant determine the correct depreciation expense of the Water Supply Systems account and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the accurate net book value of said PPE account.

We recommended that the LCE make representation with the SB for the creation of CDO position as mandated under RA No. 11535 and in accordance with existing guidelines and rules and regulations of the DILG and CSC.

We recommended that the LCE require the OIC-HRMO to:
Assess and evaluate its existing organizational structure to appropriately determine the ideal number of plantilla items/positions needed to efficiently and effectively carry out the Municipality’s operations to avoid excessive hiring of JO workers and mitigate the incurrence of additional cost;
Prepare written policies/guidelines in the hiring and selection of JO workers specifying the criteria and qualifications to be followed, and ensure consistent and equitable wage rates across the Municipality; and
Formulate a standard JO contract or similar agreement, in lieu of the existing Justification document, providing complete significant information such as, but not limited to, the wage rate, office of assignment, period and terms of employment, clear delineation of duties and responsibilities, and expected output/deliverables of the JO worker, signed and acknowledged by the JO worker, with recommendation for approval of the Department Head and approved by the LCE.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent LDF from continuing appropriation by ensuring that all funded projects are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready through continuous improvements in planning, programming, and project execution to achieve desirable socio-economic development outcomes for the community.

We recommended that the LCE require the GFPS to:
Promptly submit to the Audit Team the copy of the GPB and GAD AR, in accordance with the provision of Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001, to timely monitor and evaluate the utilization and implementation of the fund; and
Ensure that the submitted GAD AR is accompanied by the required reports as provided in Paragraph 5 of Item C.8, Section 4 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the GFPS to prepare a GPB containing attainable PPAs with more accurate fund allocation to ensure that all PPAs will be carried out in accordance with the approved plan; and
Require the Department Heads of lead or responsible offices to facilitate full implementation of PPAs assigned to them to fully utilize the allocated funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
Comply with COA rules and regulations on the posting of proper tarpaulin signboard containing complete necessary information for all infrastructure projects to be implemented;
Inform the Office of the Auditor within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure contract that the appropriate signboard is already posted onsite for Audit Team’s validation; and
Submit the list all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year at the beginning of the year as well as the quarterly report of the Publicized Government Projects/Programs/Activities of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the contractors to post warranty securities covering the required period and amount provided under Section 62.2.3 of the R-IRR of RA No. 9184, otherwise, structural defects and or failure within the warranty period not covered by the surety bond and not repaired by the contractor shall be borne by persons liable;
To ensure contractor’s compliance to the requirements of the law, require the concerned office to implement a sound and effective monitoring system to guarantee reimbursement of expenses in restoration or reconstruction work in case of Structural Defects/Failure occurring during the applicable warranty period; and
The Municipal Accountant should ensure that disbursements for final payment of completed infrastructure projects are supported with the necessary warranty security.

We recommended that Management ensure that all approved contracts for projects implemented are supported with a DOLE-approved Construction Safety and Health Program pursuant to Section 37.2.3 of the R-IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to strictly enforce the requirement for contractors to post the prescribed performance security as mandated by Section 39.1 of R-IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Management:
Remind the BAC/BAC-TWG to thoroughly verify, validate and ascertain all the statements and documents submitted by the bidder including the entries made to determine and ensure the technical competence of the winning contractors during the post-qualification evaluation; and
Ensure that all required supporting documents for the bidding documents are submitted for audit verification.

We recommended that the Management ensure that the cost calculations for all work items are in strict adherence to the DPWH DO No. 197 series of 2016. Specifically, verify that OCM and Profit are not applied to work items that are exempt from such mark-up as per the order.

We recommended that the Management:
Provide justification on the variances in the actual date of completion noted in the attached Certificate of Completion, Final Inspection Report and SWA attached to claims relative to the project Construction of Slope Protection/Riprap along Evacuation Center-Barangay Mataque, Capalonga, Camarines Norte for verification and audit action; and
Strengthen internal controls to ensure that project completion documents are issued and reviewed promptly and in accordance with the actual project completion. This could involve assigning clear responsibility for document preparation and review to avoid future discrepancies.

We recommended that the:

MA prioritize the reconciliation of the noted variance amounting ₱777,443.73 in the Trust Fund (TF) Books and prepare an adjusting entry to take up the reconciling items in the books of accounts to ensure fair presentation of the financial statements; and

Municipal Treasurer to exert effort to locate the disbursement vouchers, deposit slips and other related documents and submit the same to Office of the MA to facilitate the recording of reconciling items.

We recommended that the:

Municipal Engineer (ME) exert effort to trace and verify the status of the projects in relation to dormant CIP account balances, and submit copies of the documents such as Project Status Report and/or Certificate of Project Completion to the MA; and

MA, upon receipt of the submitted documents from the ME, make necessary adjustments in the books updating the status of the projects recorded under CIP accounts and reclassify the balance relative to all identified completed projects to appropriate asset accounts to achieve fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: ME to review the relevant documents to verify the actual status of the remaining projects outstanding in the books for more than five years.
observations.reason: Four projects under CIP account totaling ₱4,796,640.00 still remain outstanding in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MA record the adjusting entry to correct the erroneous entry made to properly recognize the receivables arising from disallowances and to achieve the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We reiterate our recommendation that the MA and MT, prospectively, promptly cause the immediate transfer of unexpended DRRMF to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) STF bank account and accurately reflect the same in the books at year-end, in conformity with Section 21 of RA No. 10121 and Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance only after the lapse of the one-year defects liability period from project completion; and

Require the BAC and the ME to establish a strong control measure to oversee the management of securities and ensure contractor's compliance with the requirements stated in Sections 62.2.3 of the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure that the Procuring Entity can recover its expenses for any necessary restoration or reconstruction works to structural defects or failures that may arise during the applicable warranty period.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.

Monitor project timelines and comply with the reportorial requirements for liquidation within the prescribed period; and

We recommend that the LCE direct the ME and MA to:
Review the validity of the outstanding Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable balance and if found to have no legitimate claim, revert the same to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund;

We recommend that the LCE:

Ensure strict adherence to the rules and regulations mandated on the proper disbursement of government funds, without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

We recommended that the LCE require the HRMO to prepare a policy, in accordance with existing rules and regulations, adopting a schedule of salary rates of JO workers based on qualification as well as the nature of work/assignment to be performed.

We recommended that the MA and MT, prospectively, cause the immediate transfer of unexpended DRRMF to the LDRRMF STF bank account and reflect the same in the books at year-end, in conformity with Section 21 of RA No. 10121 and Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the concerned offices to hasten the implementation of the partially and unimplemented PPAs under 20 per cent DF to fully attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes essential to the promotion of the general welfare of its constituents; and
Make an assessment of the not yet implemented PPA in prior year continuing appropriation to determine whether the funding for such PPAs is necessary, if not then revert to the unappropriated surplus and reprogram to development projects of utmost concern of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management:
Adhere strictly on the requirement of Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the timely submission of infrastructure contracts; and
Prospectively, (a) ensure that perfected contracts and its supporting documents are submitted within the prescribed timeframe as stated in COA Circular No. 2009-001; and (b) expedite submission of the documentary requirements of two perfected contracts and three completed infrastructure projects, for contract review and technical inspection, respectively, to facilitate examination thereof.

We recommended that LCE require the GFPS to:
Increase the allocation for GPB to a minimum of five per cent of the Annual Budget;
Establish sex-disaggregated database to assist in the identification of gender issues and concerns in the formulation of the GPB; and
Apply the HGDG tool in the evaluation and assessment of the Agency’s accomplishments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:
Require the Municipal Accountant (MA) to refrain from granting additional cash advances to officials with unliquidated balance;
Require all officials concerned to immediately refund/settle in full their outstanding cash advances; and
Require the MA to issue demand letters to accountable officials and employee with unliquidated cash advances and if still unable or persistently refuse to settle/liquidate their cash advances, resort to the withholding of salaries of Accountable Officers (AOs) and employees.

We recommended that the LCE:
Require the conduct physical count of inventory pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs and submit RPCI to the Audit Team for evaluation; and
Consider augmentation of inventory committee personnel during the conduct of actual physical count to ensure compliance with the requirements of Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs on inventory taking.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee/Property Officer prepare the required RPCPPE per PPE type in the required format reconciled with the accounting records and submit to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of the succeeding year.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the Office of the MA to determine the validity of the Accounts Payable balances amounting to ₱1,080,678.48 which are outstanding for more than two years and if found to have no legitimate claim against the Municipality, cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus;
Direct the Office of the MA to determine the obligations that do not fall within the classification criteria of current liabilities and reclassify them as non-current liabilities; and
Direct the Office of the MA and other Offices concerned to determine the causes of the non-settlement of the payables that are outstanding for more than a year and address the same.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the MA to prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted; and
Prospectively, perform a periodic review and reconciliation of the intra-agency accounts to optimize the reliability and accuracy of account balances presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management revert back to the GF the remaining unexpended CY 2016 LDRRMF balance of ₱405,353.29 to be made available for other social services, in conformity with Section 21 of RA No. 10121 and Section 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and to present a more reliable Trust Liability-DRRMF account balance.

We recommended that the LCE in coordination with the Office of the ME:
Adhere strictly on the requirement of Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the timely submission of infrastructure contracts; and
Prospectively, (a) ensure that perfected contracts and its supporting documents are submitted within the prescribed timeframe as stated in COA Circular No. 2009-001; and (b) expedite submission of the documentary requirements of one completed infrastructure project, for contract review and technical inspection, respectively, to facilitate examination thereof.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant (MA) and the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to   exert extra effort to trace the reconciling items and make necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts for fair presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statement (FS).
observations.managementAction: The MA and the MT is working hand in hand to recover the unsubmitted DVs. The MA has already adjusted some of the reconciling items.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

The need to reconcile the recorded PPE in the books with the actual PPE
We recommended the Management to:

Submit the properly accomplished Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) as prescribed by existing regulations to the accounting department and the Commission on Audit (COA) in a timely manner; and
Require the accounting department to reconcile the physical inventory with the PPE per books and adjust the records accordingly. Also, maintain corresponding Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELCs) for each property in accordance with pertinent provisions of Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS).



Unserviceable Properties

We recommended the Management to require the Disposal Committee to fast track the disposal of the unserviceable properties. After which, the disposed assets should be taken down from the books.

The necessity of titling the Land owned by the Local Government Unit (LGU)

We recommended the Management to provide necessary appropriation to facilitate the acquisition of land titles of lots of the Municipality to safeguard real properties and to document ownership of the property and in compliance with Section 449 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM), Volume I. Also, reconcile the recorded land in the books with the actual physical inventory of land to avoid overstating or possible understating of the reported asset of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Management submitted the revised RPCPPE per PPE type.

There is an initial reconciliation and adjustments done on the books, but there are few more verification that needs to be done. For the PPELCs, the assigned personnel will be trained/oriented in using the PPELCs or equivalent monitoring system for PPEs.

The newly appointed Municipal Assessor is tasked to initiate the processing for the titling of the land asset of the LGU.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

We recommended the Management to:
Strictly monitor the implementation of the projects in order to avoid delayed implementation by the winning contractors. Diligently and timely call the attention of the concerned contractor in case of noted delays and take other necessary and appropriate actions, when warranted;
Consider the forfeiture of the performance security of the concerned contractor and the enforcement of liquidated damages against the concerned contractor; and
Regularly revisit and evaluate the LGU’s compliance with the specific guidelines issued for projects funded by NGAs. In case of inadvertently overlooked non-compliance, ensure that necessary and appropriate actions are taken in accordance with the said guidelines.

We recommended that:

For projects which had already been completed, take appropriate actions relating to the return of the balance of fund transfers to the source agencies in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013 and, with regard to the savings relating to the LGU counterpart, ensure compliance with Section 418, IRR of LGC to enable the reversion of these funds to the GF; and
For not yet fully implemented projects, strictly monitor and ensure its completion as soon as possible.
The concerned Implementing Units should ensure compliance with the Implementation Schedule of the projects submitted to the SA. Should there be issues encountered during the implementation warranting the departure from such Schedule (such as, but not limited to, unavailability of supply or need for supplemental funds), such issues should be resolved timely and should be properly documented through a catch-up plan for the determination of its reasonableness; and
The LGU, primarily the LCE and the LPRAT, as well as the Implementing Units concerned, should warrant the optimal utilization of funds received and the completion of all the BUB/GPB projects.

We recommended the Management to:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to verify and validate the status of the related projects with long outstanding and unreleased retention money. When warranted, LGU should issue the corresponding Certificate of Final Acceptance of the project; and
